title: commersonia borealis
side_effects: Commersonia borealis can have side effects if used improperly, such as allergic reactions, skin irritation, and respiratory problems. Ingesting the plant's extract can cause nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea due to its high concentration of toxic compounds. Prolonged exposure to the plant's sap can lead to skin lesions, inflammation, and blistering. Improper handling of the plant can also trigger asthma attacks and other respiratory issues in sensitive individuals.
